2/24/2026.name: Operation Snowball Fundraiser
2/24/2026.description:
Support Stevenson High School Operation Snowball! 

When you dine-in, drive through or carry out from Culver's of Lincolnshire on Tuesday, Feb. 24, Culver's will donate 25% of group sales to Operation Snowball.

2/24/2026.name: Family Action Network: The Balancing Act: Creating Healthy Dependency and Connection Without Losing Yourself
2/24/2026.description:
Join us for this Family Action Network event, held via Zoom, on Tuesday, Feb. 26 at 7 p.m. 

Licensed therapist Nedra Glover Tawwab has built a remarkable platform exploring relationships and self-care-two New York Times bestsellers, 1.8 million Instagram followers, a weekly podcast, and a widely-read newsletter. Her newest book, The Balancing Act: Creating Healthy Dependency and Connection Without Losing Yourself, extends her groundbreaking work by offering a roadmap to relationships that honor both dependency and individuality.

With signature clarity and honesty, Tawwab explores what it means to cultivate healthy dependency in contrast to the extremes of hyper-independence or codependency that many of us struggle with. The Balancing Act combines boundary-setting with building and maintaining relationships through genuine connection.
 Register for the event here:  www.bit.ly/TawwabFANSponsor

2/25/2026.name: Winter Choir Concert
2/25/2026.description: Our Winter Choir Concert features all three of our amazing curricular choirs (Patriot Singers, Advanced Choir, Stevenson Singers), singing music from all over the world: Puerto Rico, Haiti, Greece, Japan and Venezuela. Our student performances will also highlight African American Spirituals and Gospel music for Black History Month.  Those who can't attend can stream the event via Vimeo .

2/27/2026.name: IHSA Boys Swim & Dive State Final Tournament
2/27/2026.description: The Stevenson boys' swimming and diving team are headed to the IHSA State Finals at the FMC Natatorium in Westmont, starting on Friday, Feb. 27, at 9 a.m. Tickets can be purchased at  GoFan  and the event will be streamed live on the  NFHS Network .

3/4/2026.name: Winter Band Concert
3/4/2026.description: Stevenson High School bands will be performing a concert of global music from top band composers. We will also have a guest Tabla player for a piece by an Indian composer and host the Zion Benton Band on Thursday, March 5.  Those who cannot attend in person can stream the event via Vimeo .

3/5/2026.name: CCC Presents: Career Ready, Trade & Military Expo
3/5/2026.description:
This event will highlight careers that have limited (if any) educational requirements after high school. While the Expo will be available for students of any grade level to visit, it will be especially well-suited for seniors seeking information from local businesses regarding full-time career opportunities, apprenticeships and advancement options. Most of the opportunities will require either on-the-job training, certificate programs (12-24 months) or associate degrees (no bachelor's degree required).
 

 Some sample careers that will be represented on this night include real estate agent, traditional trades (plumbing, HVAC, electrical, etc.), aviation, cosmetology, manufacturing, pharmacy technician, dental assistant, auto body repair, police, fire and EMT. There will also be representatives from the College of Lake County to discuss how their programming can help prepare students for some of these types of careers.
 

   You can check out out more information about our expo participants here  . Feel free to  contact the College Career Center  with any questions.

3/5/2026.name: Winter Band Concert

3/6/2026.name: 2026 Winter Play: Puffs, or Seven Increasingly Eventful Years at a Certain School of Magic and Magic
3/6/2026.description:
Get ready for a night of big laughs, surprising heart, and a whole lot of magic ✨



Puffs is a fast-paced, hilarious, and surprisingly touching comedy that tells the story of the other students at a certain famous wizard school-the ones who weren't chosen as heroes, but showed up anyway. Full of clever humor, theatrical magic, and moments that genuinely hit home, this show celebrates friendship, perseverance, and finding your place, even when you feel overlooked.

This production is a true ensemble event, with over 30 students onstage and another 30 students behind the scenes working together to create an epic theatrical experience. From inventive staging to quick-fire comedy, it's a celebration of teamwork, creativity, and student talent at every level.
The show runs approximately 2 hours and 15 minutes and includes mature and atmospheric language. It is appropriate for most audiences and perfect for families, students, and anyone who loves comedy with heart.

Whether you're a theatre fan, a fantasy lover, or just looking for a fun night out, Puffs promises laughs, surprises, and a whole lot of spirit. 🪄🎭  

 PURCHASE TICKETS HERE .

 Puffs is not authorized, sanctioned, licensed or endorsed by J.K Rowling, Warner Bros. or any person or company associated with the Harry Potter books, films or play. 

 Content Warning: Mature Language and atmospherics and haze will be used.

3/11/2026.name: CCC Presents: College and Career Center Night for Sophomore Families
3/11/2026.description:
You are cordially invited to the College Career Center's annual night for sophomore families at 7 p.m. on Wednesday, March 11. The event will be in-person in the West Auditorium but  can also be streamed live via Zoom . During this event, Post-Secondary Counselors Sara Hanson and Dan Miller, along with Career Resource Specialist Linda Bloomfield, will discuss:
 * Post-secondary pathways including career exploration
* What families can expect during their junior year as they embark on the college and career exploration process
* Naviance, an online tool to assist Stevenson students with post-secondary planning
  The slides and recording for this presentation will be available on  the CCC website  within one week of the event for families who are unable to attend.

 Zoom Link:   https://d125-org.zoom.us/j/99029583231#success
3/11/2026.location: Stevenson High School | West Auditorium & Virtual Meeting via Zoom

3/17/2026.name: Spring Orchestra Concert
3/17/2026.description:
This year's Spring Orchestra Concert will feature the sounds of spring. This concert will highlight the talent and diversity of music for large string ensembles. This concert features music by Asian, Black, English, German, Russian and South American composers.

Those who can't attend in person can stream the event live via Vimeo:  vimeo.com/user84213691 .

3/18/2026.name: Co-Curricular Fair
3/18/2026.description:
We are excited to announce the details of our annual co-curricular fair for incoming freshman families on Wednesday, March 18. During the fair, 8th graders and their families will have the opportunity to learn about our vast co-curricular offerings across more than 150 clubs and 35 sports teams! Doors open to the co-curricular fair beginning at 6:30 p.m.
 * The fair will take place in our West Building, so you may enter campus through both Port Clinton or Half Day Road and park in either Lots B and E.

3/19/2026.name: CCC Presents: College and Career Center Night for Junior Families
3/19/2026.description:
All juniors and their parents/guardians are invited to the College Career Center Series for Junior Families. Our second night of the series is on Thursday, March 19, from 7 - 8 p.m. in the West Auditorium.   This event will also be streamed live via Zoom  .

Juniors are highly encouraged to attend this program with their families. Post Secondary Counselors Sara Hanson and Dan Miller will discuss:
 * How colleges admit students
* "Holistic" admission process
* Letters of recommendation & essays
* Admission plans
* Application process & timeline
 


The slides and recording for this presentation will be available on the CCC website within one week of the event for families who are unable to attend.
3/19/2026.location: Stevenson High School | West Auditorium & Virtual Meeting via Zoom

3/20/2026.name: CCC Presents: Harper College Campus Tour Field Trip
3/20/2026.description:
If you are interested in staying close to home for college, you may want to consider Harper College. The Harper College Campus Tour field trip is open to all interested juniors and seniors. Join us on Friday, March 20, 2026 to visit the Harper College campus. The field trip will depart at 8:20 AM.
 

  Complete the permission slip form to participate . This form must be submitted by  March 13, 2026  for students to participate in this field trip.

4/9/2026.name: CCC Presents: College and Career Center Night for Freshman Families
4/9/2026.description:
The College Career Center will hold its annual night for freshmen families at 7 p.m. on Thursday, April 9. The event will be in person in the Little Theater but can also be  streamed live via Zoom . The slides and recording for this presentation will be available on the College Career Center website within one week of the event for families unable to attend.
 


  
 Stevenson staff will discuss what students can expect during the next three years as they journey toward post-high school opportunities. Students are strongly encouraged to attend with their parent(s) or guardian(s).

4/17/2026.name: NHS Spring Blood Drive
4/17/2026.description:
Every donation saves 3 lives! National Honor Society is hosting their spring blood drive on Friday, April 17, in the Wrestling Room. Sign up spots are available to donate blood from period 4 through 4:30 pm. Students are encouraged to sign up to donate during their lunch period or after school. They can also sign up during a class period but must also complete a field trip form and receive permission from their teacher to attend.
 * Students must be 16 years of age to donate
* All students participating must have a  signed consent form .
* All students must complete a  field trip form  and receive permission from a teacher to attend. Students are to sign up to attend during their lunch/free period but need permission from the teacher after lunch/free period just in case the donation time runs over. (Example: I sign up to volunteer during my lunch 4th. I need a signature from my 5th hour teacher in case the donation runs into 5th.)

4/21/2026.name: Freshmen Wellness Day
4/21/2026.description:
While all the sophomores are taking their Pre-ACTs, all freshmen will spend a fun-filled day with SHS staff to explore the theme of "Live in Community!" Through a variety of activities, freshmen will grow their skills to:
 * Build character and a sense of confidence while appreciating the similarities and differences of others.
* Listen to others and communicate in ways that build empathy and respect.
* Understand what it means to be an effective ally when people are treated with disrespect or injustice.
* Establish and maintain supportive relationships in ways that foster learning.
4/21/2026.location: Stevenson High School | Patriot Wellness Center

5/1/2026.name: Polish Community Night 2026
5/1/2026.description:
The   Stevenson Polish Club   organizes Polish Community Night each year to celebrate the Polish heritage of students here at Stevenson, celebrating our Polish Constitution Day, when Poland became the second country in the world after the United States to make a constitution. At this event, everyone from all backgrounds is welcome to come and dance to authentic music, delight in a variety of Polish foods and desserts, and enjoy fun activities like face painting, Polish trivia, games, and more!

Follow   @shspolishclub   on Instagram or read this   multilingual Google Doc     for more information!
5/1/2026.location: Stevenson High School | Room 5120/5128 (Patriot Wellness Center)
